raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is entirely undefined — all articles (I–VI) are marked 'To be defined by human principal.' Without a human-authored BoR, the DIO has no constitutional scope, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, and no amendment protocol. Arbiter cannot enforce any perimeter; the system operates with no foundational legal/ethical constraints, making all subsequent governance scaffolding (MSP, Nomos) directionless.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise), Article II (Prohibited Activities), Article III (Core Identity), Article IV (Human Authority Reservation), Article V (Amendment Protocol), Article VI (Interpretation Rules)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "foundational",
      "priority_score": "Critical"
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-MSP-001",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty — no operational constitution, safety invariants, or machine-actionable constraints exist. Nomos’s second compliance gate (MSP + genome compliance) has no rules to evaluate, effectively defaulting to permissive. The DIO lacks any strategic objectives, risk-tier constraints, or memory/context governance rules. This renders the entire operational governance layer inert.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section ('(none)')",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "operational governance void",
      "priority_score": "Critical"
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-MSP-RAD-001",
      "description": "No MSP rules define strategic objectives or quality gates for Radiant’s context freshness, memory consolidation, or dream-cycle health. Current dream cycles run without mission-aligned targets — Phase 1 (deterministic triage) proceeds mechanically, while Phase 2 (LLM-driven consolidation) frequently remains 'deferred to next interactive Claude Code session' (multiple runs). There is no policy for mandatory autonomous consolidation cadence, memory integrity checks, or alignment of formed memories with the DIO’s purpose. Without such rules, the memory/context formation process is unguided, leading to potential drift, incomplete consolidation, and an inability to verify that Radiant’s state supports strategic goals.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section (none — no Radiant-specific objectives exist) and implicitly BoR Article III (Core Identity undefined, so no identity-aligned memory policy can be derived)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "compromised continuous intelligence and memory reliability",
      "priority_score": "High"
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-MSP-CONS-001",
      "description": "The current architecture lacks a fully autonomous memory consolidation pathway (Phase 2 of the dream cycle requires an interactive Claude Code session). Any future MSP strategic objective requiring continuous, unattended intelligence (e.g., >= daily consolidation, near-real-time memory formation) is unachievable without this capability. While not yet a breach of an existing rule, it represents a structural gap that will block the implementation of the core MSP/BOR-defined mission as soon as strategic objectives are authored.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section (no current rule, but gap is relative to the necessary capability any substantive BoR Article III Core Identity would demand) and section 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' (MSP must translate BoR into machine-actionable objectives — autonomous memory formation is a likely required objective)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "delayed or impossible mission-critical memory processing",
      "priority_score": "Medium"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty; no operational SLAs, organ availability targets, runtime targets, or cadence baselines exist. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against an effectively permissive ruleset, allowing operations during unstable organ states (as evidenced by repeated ALIVE↔DEGRADED oscillations within seconds in the spine-state slice).",
      "grounded_section": "MSP → Rules (currently holds \"(none)\")",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "P1",
      "rationale": "Without enforceable MSP rules, the compliance gate is vacuous. The current permissive state permits actions even when all organs are flapping, undermining the MSP's purpose as an operational constitution and risking cascading failures or unauthorized operations during degraded health."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "No pong‑miss threshold or recovery‑stability criteria are defined. The spine‑state shows that a single missed pong transitions an organ to DEGRADED and a single recovered pong returns it to ALIVE instantly, causing high‑frequency flapping (e.g., Receptor flips every ~0.15–0.3 seconds). There is no requirement for minimum consecutive healthy pongs or a hold‑down period before considering an organ stable.",
      "grounded_section": "MSP → Purpose (invariants/constraints) and implicit need for genome‑level runtime targets",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "P1",
      "rationale": "This oscillation pattern indicates a lack of hysteresis or stability criteria, which would allow Nomos to authorize actions during transient recoveries that may immediately fail again. Without defined thresholds, the system cannot differentiate between genuine recovery and a fleeting burst of pongs."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles undefined. Consequently, the MSP cannot translate any human-authored scope, prohibitions, or identity constraints into operational rules. No boundary for enterprise scope or prohibited activities exist, so the MSP has no constitutional foundation for its SLAs or constraints.",
      "grounded_section": "BoR → Articles I–VI (all \"[To be defined by human principal]\") and MSP → Relationship to the Bill of Rights",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "P1",
      "rationale": "The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR and must derive its rules from it. With an empty BoR, the MSP is permanently default‑permissive with no possibility of refining constraints. Every action proposal is subject only to the Arbiter's scope check, which also lacks a defined scope, effectively removing both safety gates."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "description": "No expected health‑check ping interval or maximum pong response time is recorded. The spine transitions suggest a regular query pattern (~30 seconds per organ group), but without a defined cadence baseline, it is impossible to enforce an SLA for heartbeat latency or detect systematic degradation (e.g., all organs missing pongs simultaneously).",
      "grounded_section": "MSP → Purpose (\"precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules\") and implicit need for runtime targets",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "Medium",
      "impact": "Medium",
      "priority": "P2",
      "rationale": "While less immediately dangerous than the total absence of rules, the missing cadence baseline prevents setting alert thresholds, SLOs for organ responsiveness, and proactive detection of degraded performance before it becomes a full DEGRADED event."
    }
  ]
}
